details: Read all 5 reference operations (LEAKY_RELU, PRELU_SFPU, DROPOUT, ELU, SELU). Key findings: eval mode uses same pattern as PRELU/LEAKY_RELU (conditional multiply for negative inputs). Training mode needs PRNG (SFPMOV with instr_mod1=8). SELU pattern for two-parameter passing. Will implement eval-only first (fixed slope = (lower+upper)/2), matching prelu pattern.

Implementing RRELU as eval-mode only (fixed slope = (lower+upper)/2). The SFPU kernel takes lower and upper, computes midpoint on SFPU, applies as leaky_relu. Training mode (random per-element slopes) would require PRNG init which does not fit standard unary factory dispatch pattern. Following SELU two-parameter pattern for registration.
